jokes.text: Just in time for the all-you-can-stand buffet at Chizzler.
jokes.setup: Character mentions meeting for dinner at what sounds like a restaurant
jokes.punchline: The restaurant name plays on 'all-you-can-eat' but substitutes 'stand' suggesting the food is barely tolerable
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - sounds like a real restaurant chain but implies questionable food quality

jokes.text: Ew! Hit it with a stick!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg introduces his video date as 'delightful creature'
jokes.punchline: Crew's immediate disgust and suggestion to hit her with a stick
jokes.explanation: Strong reaction comedy that establishes the crew's typical response to Zoidberg's romantic interests

jokes.text: There. My home's all clean except for one part, the inside.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg claims he's straightening up his place for the date
jokes.punchline: Reveals he only cleaned the outside, inside is still filthy
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist logic delivered with Zoidberg's characteristic misunderstanding of basic concepts. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Aww. I'm sure it's fine by the standards of a gross alien monster.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to be supportive about Zoidberg's cleaning
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that references his alien nature as an excuse for low standards
jokes.explanation: Typical Leela response - trying to be kind but accidentally insulting. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Nope. Step aside, people. I got bug bombs.
jokes.setup: Leela suggests Zoidberg's place might be acceptable
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately contradicts with extreme pest control measures
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender solution - immediate escalation to violence/destruction. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Not my problem. Fire in the hole!
jokes.setup: Fry asks if bugs will just infest their building instead
jokes.punchline: Bender's complete lack of concern for consequences
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender selfishness. Character-driven but predictable.

jokes.text: You move like a young, roach-infested Gene Kelly.
jokes.setup: Complimenting Bender's dancing ability
jokes.punchline: Compares him to Gene Kelly but adds the qualifier 'roach-infested'
jokes.explanation: Unexpected modifier on a classic compliment. The roach infestation ties to the bug bombing scene.

jokes.text: If you don't enter, I'll just kill myself, okay? Okay. No. Wait.
jokes.setup: Randy threatens to kill himself if Bender doesn't enter contest
jokes.punchline: Bender casually agrees before realizing that's wrong
jokes.explanation: Bender's sociopathic response followed by quick correction. Good timing and character work.

jokes.text: At last, a chance to realize my brand-new dream of being the greatest tap dancer in all the world. Well, in a three-block radius. Of all time!
jokes.setup: Bender declares his new dream to be the greatest tap dancer
jokes.punchline: Immediately scales back to three-block radius, then escalates again
jokes.explanation: Great escalation/de-escalation/re-escalation pattern. Very Bender-like grandiosity tempered by reality.

jokes.text: I need something beautiful and cheap for a lady who is one of those things.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg asking for flowers that are beautiful and cheap
jokes.punchline: Implies his date is only one of those qualities (cheap)
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor about his date while revealing his cheapness. Good economy.

jokes.text: Perfect! I'll take half a bouquet of daisies for $3.
jokes.setup: Offered cheapest flowers for $6
jokes.punchline: Wants to buy literally half of the cheapest option
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg cheapness taken to absurd lengths. Predictable but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I'm gonna snip her off her feet!
jokes.setup: Context of flower cutting and upcoming date
jokes.punchline: Malapropism mixing 'sweep her off her feet' with 'snip' from flower cutting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg malapropism that connects to the immediate flower-cutting context. Very quotable.

jokes.text: You're even more beautiful from a distance!
jokes.setup: Romantic reunion greeting
jokes.punchline: Accidentally implies she looks worse up close
jokes.explanation: Classic accidental insult disguised as compliment. Perfect Zoidberg social awkwardness.

jokes.text: It's like Comic-Con in a submarine!
jokes.setup: Describing a horrible stench
jokes.punchline: Specific comparison that combines two things associated with bad smells
jokes.explanation: Highly specific and visual comparison that's both funny and accurate. Very quotable pop culture reference.

jokes.text: That's just my personal musk. You'll learn to love it. I know I did.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg's date complaining about his smell
jokes.punchline: His casual attitude and admission he had to learn to love his own smell
jokes.explanation: Reveals Zoidberg had to get used to his own smell, adding layers to the character.

jokes.text: Something's come up. It's vomit!
jokes.setup: Polite euphemism for breaking up
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals it's literally vomit
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with economy. Subverts expectation of polite breakup excuse.

jokes.text: You are a living thing. Right? Uh-huh. Can I have a refund now?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg says he can't stand to see living things in pain
jokes.punchline: Marianne immediately tries to exploit his sympathy for a refund
jokes.explanation: Shows Marianne's opportunistic nature and sets up her character.

jokes.text: This here's a bayonet. It's some kind of kooky half gun, half knife.
jokes.setup: Armed robbery with bayonet
jokes.punchline: Robber's folksy, confused description of a bayonet
jokes.explanation: Unexpected characterization of robber as folksy and not too bright. 'Kooky' is perfectly chosen word.

jokes.text: And no bag, please. I care about the environment.
jokes.setup: Armed robber demanding money
jokes.punchline: Shows environmental consciousness during the crime
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of criminal behavior with environmental consciousness. Very original character beat.

jokes.text: Don't you get it? I'm crazy!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg confronting armed robber
jokes.punchline: His explanation for his bravery is that he's crazy
jokes.explanation: Logical Zoidberg reasoning - his life is so bad that danger doesn't matter.

jokes.text: That ain't legal. Ain't you heard of no Geneva Convention?
jokes.setup: Robber encountering Zoidberg's smell
jokes.punchline: Criminal complaining about legality and citing Geneva Convention
jokes.explanation: Robber invoking international law about chemical weapons while committing armed robbery. Great irony.

jokes.text: I like the way you hit! But I don't get it. Most women I've met can't even stand next to me without collapsing.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment after the robbery
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's confusion about why she doesn't collapse from his smell
jokes.explanation: Sets up the mystery while staying true to Zoidberg's romantic inexperience.

jokes.text: Well, some of them didn't have legs.
jokes.setup: Explaining why some women couldn't stand next to him
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation - they physically couldn't stand
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect literal interpretation. Zoidberg's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I'm smellier than a whorehouse's outhouse!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg trying to explain his smell problem delicately
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude and specific comparison
jokes.explanation: Crude but vivid metaphor that fits Zoidberg's tendency toward inappropriate analogies.

jokes.text: The previous tenant was a very prominent raccoon.
jokes.setup: Marianne complimenting his dumpster
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact description of raccoon as 'prominent'
jokes.explanation: Dignifies a raccoon with social status. Absurd but delivered seriously.

jokes.text: Too bad he got partially run over by that steamroller.
jokes.setup: Discussion of prominent raccoon
jokes.punchline: 'Partially' suggests incomplete steamroller job
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with the modifier 'partially' making it funnier and more disturbing.

jokes.text: Ouch! I forgot I was a giant crab!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg pinching himself to see if he's dreaming
jokes.punchline: Realizes his crab claws make self-pinching painful
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific physical comedy. Only Zoidberg could forget his own anatomy.

jokes.text: After weeks of searching, I'm proud to introduce the five people willing to participate.
jokes.setup: Introducing dance contest participants
jokes.punchline: Admits these are just the only people willing, not the best
jokes.explanation: Undercuts the grandeur of competition by revealing low participation. Honest but deflating.

jokes.text: The extra clicking you'll hear is gonna be my hip.
jokes.setup: Elderly tap dancer being introduced
jokes.punchline: Pre-explains clicking sounds from his failing hip
jokes.explanation: Age-appropriate humor that's specific to tap dancing context. Good setup for physical limitations.

jokes.text: She's always stealing them! Also, she has a serious heart condition.
jokes.setup: Explaining heart condition reference
jokes.punchline: First jokes about stealing hearts, then reveals actual medical condition
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays with the double meaning before getting serious.

jokes.text: I'm six years old, and tap dancing is my favoritest thing, even if it's not good for my pacemaker.
jokes.setup: Cute six-year-old talking about her hobby
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions pacemaker risk
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from innocent child. The casual mention of medical danger is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: That is one quality pacemaker.
jokes.setup: Child's pacemaker visible/audible
jokes.punchline: Bender's technical appreciation for medical device
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - focusing on the technology rather than the medical situation.

jokes.text: Oh, there is no prize, Dorothy, unless you count the satisfaction of winning.
jokes.setup: Bender asking about grand prize
jokes.punchline: Reveals there's no actual prize, just satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Standard comedy trope about meaningless competitions. 'Dorothy' reference adds slight character.

jokes.text: Out of my way, fatty! I'm practicing. Whoa!
jokes.setup: Bender being rude while practicing
jokes.punchline: Immediately falls or messes up while being arrogant
jokes.explanation: Immediate comeuppance for Bender's arrogance. Predictable but satisfying.

jokes.text: She's not a hologram. Not a robot-slave. We give up.
jokes.setup: Crew trying to figure out what's wrong with Zoidberg's girlfriend
jokes.punchline: List of increasingly specific sci-fi relationship problems
jokes.explanation: Good world-building humor that references typical sci-fi relationship problems.

jokes.text: We had a wonderful night of love-making in my dumpster. Ew!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg sharing romantic details
jokes.punchline: Both the location and Amy's immediate disgust
jokes.explanation: TMI moment with predictable reaction. The dumpster location adds character specificity.

jokes.text: And I, as a renowned ear, nose, and blowhole doctor, could do the operation!
jokes.setup: Discussion of nose transplant surgery
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's medical specialty includes 'blowhole' for sea creatures
jokes.explanation: Perfect world-building detail about alien medicine. 'Blowhole' is unexpected addition to medical specialty.

jokes.text: Listen to Orange Joe.
jokes.setup: Hermes giving advice about the relationship
jokes.punchline: Refers to himself in third person with weird nickname
jokes.explanation: Random self-nickname that sounds like a wrestler or DJ. Typical Hermes odd behavior.

jokes.text: I'm gonna kick your ass, little girl.
jokes.setup: Sweet six-year-old expressing joy about dancing
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate threat to a child
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - inappropriate threats against innocent child. Character-true but predictable.

jokes.text: You can brew it into a tea if you like severe stomach cramps.
jokes.setup: Describing beautiful flower properties
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of painful side effects
jokes.explanation: Dry delivery of painful consequences. Good subversion of romantic flower talk.

jokes.text: It's like summer, with a dab of vanilla and honey, or maybe winter with some gravy on it.
jokes.setup: Trying to describe flower scents to Marianne
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical and food-obsessed description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg logic - associates everything with food. 'Winter with gravy' is particularly absurd.

jokes.text: I've never known anyone who wanted to smell so badly,
jokes.setup: Zoidberg talking about Marianne's desire for smell
jokes.punchline: Double meaning - wanting to smell vs. smelling badly
jokes.explanation: Classic wordplay that works in context. Zoidberg wouldn't realize the double meaning.

jokes.text: I want the biggest nose you can find!
jokes.setup: Excited about getting sense of smell
jokes.punchline: Wants maximum nose size for maximum smelling ability
jokes.explanation: Logical enthusiasm taken to extreme. Shows her eagerness for sensory experience.

jokes.text: So right before she dances, I'll sneak in and put rusty tacks in them. The harder she dances, the more she bleeds.
jokes.setup: Bender planning to cheat in dance contest
jokes.punchline: Horrifically violent plan against six-year-old
jokes.explanation: Extremely dark plan that's shocking in its detail. Pure Bender sociopathy.

jokes.text: Once Tonya drops out due to blood loss, I kidnap her parents and make them watch while I cut off her hair with these hedge clippers.
jokes.setup: Already horrific plan involving child
jokes.punchline: Escalates to kidnapping and psychological torture
jokes.explanation: Escalation to absurd levels of villainy. The psychological torture aspect pushes it over the top.

jokes.text: I've looked into her eyes and seen her soul! She's a monster.
jokes.setup: Fry defending the sweet little girl as an 'angel'
jokes.punchline: Reveals he actually sees her as evil
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection. Sets up defense of child then reveals she's actually evil. Great structure.

jokes.text: The championship's mine, robot.
jokes.setup: Six-year-old girl in competition
jokes.punchline: Threatening tone from innocent child
jokes.explanation: Confirms the 'monster' assessment. Child using intimidating language is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: She'll be forever grateful from somewhere upwind.
jokes.setup: Discussion of giving Marianne nose surgery
jokes.punchline: She'll have to stay upwind due to Zoidberg's smell
jokes.explanation: Logical consequence humor. Good economy and practical joke about wind direction.

jokes.text: But you know what would really stink? If Marianne spent her whole life never knowing the beautiful scent of her own flowers.
jokes.setup: Discussing the moral dilemma of the surgery
jokes.punchline: 'Stink' used metaphorically in conversation about smell
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that works in context. Zoidberg's genuine sentiment comes through.

jokes.text: Here, patch yourself up with this medicinal wacky pack.
jokes.setup: Bender needs medical attention
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg offers clearly inadequate medical treatment
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg medical incompetence. 'Wacky pack' sounds like toy more than medicine.

jokes.text: There was an accident at the fencing academy. Touché.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg has donor nose from accident
jokes.punchline: Uses fencing term 'touché' for actual sword accident
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting fencing terminology to actual injury. Good dark humor.

jokes.text: Smell you later.
jokes.setup: Surgery to give Marianne sense of smell
jokes.punchline: Common phrase 'see you later' changed to smell reference
jokes.explanation: Simple but appropriate pun for the context. Works as goodbye before surgery.

jokes.text: Break a leg, Bender. Oops! Looks like you already did.
jokes.setup: Traditional good luck phrase for performers
jokes.punchline: Six-year-old's fake concern over literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Evil child delivering fake sympathy. Good use of theater terminology.

jokes.text: In another life, we could've been friends. No chance, garbage-can face.
jokes.setup: Bender finding common ground with evil child
jokes.punchline: Child immediately rejects any friendship
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing child is even more evil than Bender. 'Garbage-can face' is appropriately childish insult.

jokes.text: It's time for some fancy-shmancy dancy.
jokes.setup: Introducing the dance-off
jokes.punchline: Alliterative phrase mixing fancy/dance
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that fits Randy's enthusiastic announcer character. Not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You know, the doctors say my heart doesn't work very good, but it feels good today, 'cause I got to dance and make people happy.
jokes.setup: Sweet victory speech from six-year-old
jokes.punchline: Innocent delivery contrasts with her established evil nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between innocent words and established evil character. Makes her more unsettling.

jokes.text: I never knew I could hate this much! She's a pig! I'm gonna tap-dance on her grave some day!
jokes.setup: Bender losing to six-year-old
jokes.punchline: Extreme hatred and death threats against child
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender overreaction. The grave dancing threat ties to his tap dancing obsession.

jokes.text: A cute little heart attack!
jokes.setup: Child having medical emergency
jokes.punchline: Characterizing heart attack as 'cute'
jokes.explanation: Absurd characterization of medical emergency. The word choice is perfectly inappropriate.

jokes.text: I'm a doctor! and, yes, they're real.
jokes.setup: Medical emergency requiring doctor
jokes.punchline: Female doctor preemptively addressing breast questions
jokes.explanation: Quick throwaway joke about predictable male reaction. Good economy.

jokes.text: You smell just like I dreamed you would.
jokes.setup: Marianne can now smell Zoidberg's terrible odor
jokes.punchline: Claims he smells like her dreams
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation that she'd be repulsed. Shows her genuine love transcends smell.

jokes.text: I never learned a good smell from a bad smell, but I like the way you smell, because I like you.
jokes.setup: Explanation of her acceptance of his smell
jokes.punchline: Love transcends sensory experience
jokes.explanation: Sweet resolution that's both funny and touching. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: In your dead face, little girl! I'm not even gonna wait to dance on your grave. I'm gonna dance on your corpse right now.
jokes.setup: Bender thinks Tonya is dead
jokes.punchline: Immediate corpse dancing instead of waiting for burial
jokes.explanation: Escalates his earlier threat to immediate action. Horrifically inappropriate but funny.

jokes.text: I took a nappie!
jokes.setup: Everyone thinks she died
jokes.punchline: Childish explanation that she was just napping
jokes.explanation: Perfect child-like explanation. 'Nappie' is perfectly childish word choice.

jokes.text: Bender's rhythmic chest compressions restarted her heart.
jokes.setup: Bender dancing on 'corpse'
jokes.punchline: His dancing accidentally performed CPR
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal where evil intent becomes lifesaving. Perfect ironic structure.

jokes.text: Just 'cause you saved my life doesn't mean I won't beat you down again. Together, we shall rule the galaxy!
jokes.setup: Bender saved Tonya's life
jokes.punchline: She threatens him then proposes evil partnership
jokes.explanation: Perfect evil child logic - ungrateful but recognizes kindred spirit. Galaxy domination is perfectly scaled.

jokes.text: Listen, I've got a new job, and, well, I'm going to have to dump you.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg talking to Marianne in his dumpster home
jokes.punchline: 'Dump' works both as breakup and literal location
jokes.explanation: Perfect double meaning that works both literally and romantically. Great economy.

jokes.text: I'd like that!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg trying to break up with her
jokes.punchline: She takes it as invitation to the literal dump
jokes.explanation: Perfect misunderstanding that saves the relationship. Great payoff to the pun setup.
